rulings.Case Title: GARY SHORT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 14, 2007, LINE 5 DEFENDANT SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Hearing required. The Court's tentative decision is to grant the motion. It appears that the decision in Miller v. Los Angeles Cty. Flood Control Dist. (1973) 8 Cal.3d 689 requires expert testimony to establish Bechtel's liability for the design and construction of the Antioch plant and that plaintiff's claim, therefore, must be treated as one for professional negligence. However, the fact that a CCP § 411.35 certificate was not filed is not determinative on this record for no motion for judgment on the pleadings has been filed. The decision in Dow v. Holly Manufacturing Company (1958) 49 C.2d 720 extends liability to a general contractor, no longer in control of the property, for latent defects. However, plaintiff's theory of liability is that asbestos is not a latent defect, but one that was well known in the 1960's and is the reason that Bechtel breached the GISO standards when it designed and constructed the plant. The questions regarding the Cohen Declaration will be addressed at the hearing. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WILSON vs MAXXAM, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 14, 2007, LINE 10 DEFENDANT CHARLES HURWITZ, MAXXAM INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Overruled; ten days to answer. The Court does not find that the claims at issue are subject to the public disclosure limitation. The issue of exhaustion of internal remedies is a question of fact, not for demurrer. On the face of the complaint, it is not apparent that the statute of limitations bars the claims. The Court does not find persuasive defendants' arguments that there was no "claim" and that SYP was not essential to the purchase. Finally, fraud and alter ego are sufficiently plead. =(302/PJM/PB)
